page.content: In the analysis of therapeutic outcomes, Table 1 presents the effects of varying TQ Daily Dose (mg/kg/day × 3) on patient recovery, showing for TQ Alone and TQ + CQ (16 mg/kg/day × 3) the number of patients cured and not cured in percentage. At a dose of 0.06, TQ Alone resulted in 0/2 (0%) cured, while TQ + CQ showed 0/2 (0%). At 0.2, TQ Alone again resulted in 0/2 (0%)*, with TQ + CQ at 0/2 (0%). The 0.6 mg/kg/day × 3 dose for TQ Alone had 0/2 (0%)* cured and TQ + CQ achieved 2/0 (100%). At a dose of 2, TQ Alone showed 1/1 (50%) cured, while TQ + CQ again achieved 2/0 (100%). Finally, at 6 mg/kg/day × 3, TQ Alone had 2/0 (100%) cured, and TQ + CQ was not applicable.

page.content: In Table 2, demographic data reveals a total number of patients (n = 267) with a median age at diagnosis of 35 (13–85) years. Among these, premenopausal women accounted for 213 (80%), and postmenopausal women for 52 (20%). The median age of premenopausal women was 32 (13–55) years, while that of postmenopausal women was 62 (48–85) years.
